summary: The diff implements most of the required features: a full admin dashboard with three tabs, backend admin functions, admin login page improvements, and protected routes. However, REQ-1 (remove Caffeine branding) is only partially addressed — the footer still contains a caffeine.ai attribution link. The backend implements most REQ-2 functions but the diff evidence for 'getAllUsers' uses 'listUsers' instead, and 'getSiteConfig'/'setSiteConfig' naming may differ. Site config stable storage persistence is not clearly evidenced. The Submission Moderation tab is missing a timestamp column. Scan count per user is not properly implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Remove any remaining watermark or Caffeine branding from all visible UI surfaces, including the landing page, scan dashboard, results page, header, and footer.
missing_requirements.reason: The Footer.tsx still contains a caffeine.ai attribution link with UTM tracking parameters. The file-summaries.txt also explicitly notes the footer has a 'required caffeine.ai attribution link'. This violates the AC: 'Footer displays only Truth-Lens copyright and tagline' and 'No Caffeine logo, badge, or watermark text is visible on any page'.

missing_requirements.requirement: Site configuration is persisted in stable storage.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend main.mo shows siteConfig declared as a regular Map (let siteConfig = Map.empty<Text, Text>()) with no stable variable declaration or stable storage annotation visible in the truncated diff, which may not survive canister upgrades.

missing_requirements.requirement: Submission Moderation tab shows all scan submissions with verdict badges, confidence scores, timestamps, and a delete button per row.
missing_requirements.reason: The SubmissionModerationTab.tsx table header shows Content, Verdict, Confidence, and Actions columns but no Timestamp column. The ScanResult type also does not appear to include a timestamp field.

missing_requirements.requirement: User Management tab shows a sortable table with each user's name, principal ID, scan count, and action buttons to ban or delete.
missing_requirements.reason: The getScanCount function in UserManagementTab.tsx returns scanResults.length for all users (the total count), rather than a per-user scan count. The backend does not track scans per user and there is no per-user scan count derivation logic, so the scan count column does not reflect accurate per-user data.

hallucinated_features.description: The backend function for listing users is named 'listUsers' in the frontend queries, while the build request specifies 'getAllUsers' as the required function name.
hallucinated_features.reason: useGetAllUsers hook calls actor.listUsers() but REQ-2 AC specifies the function must be named getAllUsers.

hallucinated_features.description: The SiteConfigurationTab reads config values using actor.readConfigValue() rather than getSiteConfig as specified in REQ-2.
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-2 AC specifies getSiteConfig and setSiteConfig as the required admin function names, but the frontend calls readConfigValue for reading.
